h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the cart rolls left while the hand pushes right, this card still stays with the right-hand arrow?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was trying to reconcile how a single force label could apply when the cart's motion opposed the force's direction.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [puts the contact strip under the rightward arrow and moves the rolling-left strip into the side space] Under the arrow: hand on the cart, pushing right. The rolling-left sentence goes over here, because it says what the cart does, not what is acting on it.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new spatial sorting mechanism, physically separating the interaction strip from the motion strip to demonstrate the new criterion.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names the limitation of the prior card-placing route and introduces a new physical sorting mechanism with distinct spatial zones to separate force from motion.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific and directly resolves the learner's cognitive blockage by externalizing the distinction between interaction and motion into separate physical locations.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3.
